{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents discuss the mechanics of tooth extraction and root movement. The focus is on applying forces to the teeth to sever the periodontal ligament and move the tooth. The key is to apply a force that is effective in moving the tooth without causing damage to surrounding structures. Positive intermittent force would be too sporadic and inconsistent for precise root movement. Torque or moment is not mentioned in the context of root movement. Extra-oral force is not applicable as the force needs to be applied to the tooth itself. Gentle action aligns with the need to apply a controlled force to avoid iatrogenic injury and ensure successful root movement.", "answer_choice": "D"}
